title: Freshly Fluffy Irish Tamarind Pilaf
description: A fresh and fluffy brunch dish featuring tamarind paste, a classic ingredient in Irish cuisine, with raw-friendly modifications for an exciting twist.

ingredients.name: 2 cups water or low-sodium vegetable broth
Freshly squeezed lemon juice, to taste

instructions: Rinse quinoa under cold water and rinse until the water runs clear. Combine with water or broth in a medium saucepan.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until quinoa is tender and most of the liquid has been absorbed, about 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, in a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onion and garlic; sauté for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in cumin and smoked paprika (if using). Cook for an additional minute to toast spices.
Add tamarind paste to the skillet. Stir well to combine with onions, ensuring there are no lumps. Gradually pour in water or broth used to cook quinoa.
Bring mixture back to a simmer. Once boiling, reduce heat and let it simmer for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
Stir in lemon juice until the sauce is smooth and slightly thickened. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Divide cooked quinoa among serving bowls and ladle tamarind sauce over each portion. Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.
